scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: 12 read streams (the wave2 legacy-parity projects stream plus users, case_types, case_fields, priorities, statuses, result_fields, templates as global reference streams, and suites/milestones/cases/plans/runs fanned out per-project via the engine's single-level fan_out dialect) and 10 write actions covering project/milestone/suite/case/plan/run/result create-update-lifecycle mutations. Excluded: sections/tests/results/configurations (each requires a SECOND path parameter sourced from a per-project child resource id - e.g. get_sections needs project_id+suite_id, get_tests/get_results need a run_id/test_id discovered only by first listing runs for a project - and the engine's fan_out dialect is single-level only, ENGINE_GAP, not a workaround); attachment binary endpoints; BDD/shared-step template bodies; bulk/CSV/variable/dataset endpoints gated behind TestRail Enterprise-tier feature flags this bundle cannot detect at authoring time; and non-data/admin endpoints (get_reports, delete_project, groups CRUD).

excluded.reason: single-project detail; identical record shape to the projects list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
project-settings update (name/announcement/suite_mode); Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage, add_project already covers project-creation parity
irreversibly deletes a project and every suite/case/run/result nested under it; too destructive for an unreviewed reverse-ETL action
single-user detail; identical record shape to the users list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
same user record shape as the users stream, looked up by email instead of id; not a distinct resource
authenticated-caller identity introspection, not a syncable object stream
same status vocabulary as get_statuses, restricted to the subset legal for a case's own status field; not a distinct resource shape
single-suite detail; identical record shape to the suites stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
suite name/description update; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage, add_suite already covers suite-creation parity
irreversibly deletes a suite and every section/case nested under it

requires BOTH project_id and suite_id path/query parameters; the engine's fan_out dialect resolves exactly one id level per stream (single-level ids_from), and suite_id is itself only discoverable by first fanning out over projects to list suites - a second, nested fan-out level the engine does not support. ENGINE_GAP, not a per-connector workaround.
single-section detail; same nested-fan-out ENGINE_GAP as get_sections above

section creation targets a project_id+suite_id pair; without a modeled sections read stream (ENGINE_GAP above) there is no discovered section_id to round-trip through a parity read, so this write is deferred alongside its read counterpart
same nested-resource ENGINE_GAP as add_section
irreversibly deletes a section and every case nested under it
single-case detail; identical record shape to the cases stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
bulk multi-case update over the same collection update_case already covers per-record; not a distinct mutation shape this dialect needs a second action for
irreversibly deletes a test case and its full result history across every run that ever included it
bulk irreversible case deletion; strictly more destructive than the already-excluded single-case delete
bulk case-copy admin operation, not a plain create/update/delete mutation shape; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
bulk case-move admin operation, not a plain create/update/delete mutation shape; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
per-case audit-log/change-history feed, not a syncable primary object stream; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
single-milestone detail; identical record shape to the milestones stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
milestone field update (name/description/due_on/is_completed); Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage, add_milestone already covers milestone-creation parity
irreversibly deletes a milestone; unlinks every case/run/plan that referenced it
single-plan detail (with nested plan-entry/run breakdown); identical top-level record shape to the plans stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
adds a nested run-group entry inside an existing plan; a compound sub-resource write beyond this dialect's single-level create/update/delete shape, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
plan field update; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage, add_plan already covers plan-creation parity
nested plan-entry update; same compound-sub-resource shape as add_plan_entry
plan lifecycle close; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage, close_run already covers the equivalent run-level lifecycle action
irreversibly deletes a test plan and every run/result nested under it
irreversibly deletes a run-group entry and its results from a plan
single-run detail; identical record shape to the runs stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
run field update (name/description/case selection); Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage, add_run already covers run-creation parity

requires a run_id path parameter discoverable only by first fanning out over projects to list runs - a second, nested fan-out level the engine's single-level fan_out dialect does not support. ENGINE_GAP, not a per-connector workaround; same shape as the sections gap above.
single-test detail; same nested-fan-out ENGINE_GAP as get_tests above

requires a test_id path parameter, itself only discoverable via get_tests/{run_id} - a THIRD nesting level (project -> run -> test) beyond the engine's single-level fan_out dialect. ENGINE_GAP, not a per-connector workaround.
requires both a run_id (nested fan-out ENGINE_GAP, see get_tests above) and a case_id; same underlying gap
requires a run_id path parameter, same nested-fan-out ENGINE_GAP as get_tests above

targets a test_id that is only discoverable via the excluded get_tests/{run_id} read (ENGINE_GAP); add_result_for_case (run_id+case_id, both already-known ids from the runs and cases streams) covers the equivalent parity-testable mutation shape instead
bulk multi-test-id result submission over the same run add_result_for_case already covers per-case; not a distinct shape this dialect needs a second action for
bulk multi-case-id result submission over the same run add_result_for_case already covers per-case; not a distinct shape this dialect needs a second action for
test-run configuration groups/variants (browser/OS matrices); a niche run-scoping feature not every TestRail plan uses, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
same configuration-group feature surface excluded above
same configuration-group feature surface excluded above
same configuration-group feature surface excluded above
same configuration-group feature surface excluded above
irreversibly deletes a configuration group and unlinks every run/plan-entry that referenced it
irreversibly deletes a single configuration variant
user-group membership listing, an access-control admin resource rather than test-management data; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
same access-control admin resource excluded above
user-group/permission management requires TestRail admin-tier API access beyond ordinary project read/write credentials
same admin-tier permission scope required as add_group
irreversibly deletes a user group and its permission assignments
lists configured report templates, not a syncable data object stream
triggers report generation and returns a PDF/HTML report artifact URL, not a JSON record stream
lists attachment metadata whose payload is a binary file download, not a syncable JSON record stream
same binary-attachment shape as get_attachments_for_case
same binary-attachment shape as get_attachments_for_case
same binary-attachment shape as get_attachments_for_case
same binary-attachment shape as get_attachments_for_case
downloads the raw attachment file bytes, not a JSON record
multipart binary file upload; this dialect's body_type dialect (json/form/none) has no multipart shape
same multipart-upload shape as add_attachment_to_case
same multipart-upload shape as add_attachment_to_case
same multipart-upload shape as add_attachment_to_case
same multipart-upload shape as add_attachment_to_case
deletes a binary attachment resource; grouped with the rest of the binary-attachment surface excluded this pass
BDD (Gherkin) scenario template text for a case, a niche opt-in template feature; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
same BDD-template feature surface excluded above
reusable shared-step library, a niche opt-in feature most projects do not use; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
same shared-steps feature surface excluded above
same shared-steps feature surface excluded above
same shared-steps feature surface excluded above
